title: diphasiastrum complanatum subsp. montellii
intro: Diphasiastrum complanatum subsp. montellii, also known as Montell's forked polypody, is a rare subspecies of the Diphasiastrum complanatum fern, native to the Appalachian Mountains in eastern North America. This plant is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Diphasiastrum complanatum subsp. montellii are said to include relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as wound healing and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, although its use and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
